topic: actor visibility filtering at selection confirmation
decision_taken: wave-1 store has no access/disclosure model, so confirmObservationAddSelection's viewable-only filter admits every selected asset; the consume-exactly-once and silent-omission SHAPE is preserved, the filter is inert
reversal_condition: a disclosure/access model lands in the shared store (role/permission work is deferred by the wave plan)

decision_taken: no mutator in the contract can set quantity configuration, so the materialized configuration is empty and the projection answers null (the contract's permitted branch)
reversal_condition: a quantity-configuration event kind is added to the shared taxonomy

decision_taken: the add-log selection workflow is modeled as two feature-registered kinds; consumed-exactly-once is enforced at confirm time by scanning for a prior selection_confirmed
reversal_condition: another feature needs the same selection workflow (promote to shared taxonomy)

decision_taken: listObservationRevisions is DERIVED from the event log (created + one revision per quantity deletion carrying the deleted quantity id) rather than stored as revision entities; Drupal new_revision storage idiom is excluded per scope
reversal_condition: a fixture asserting revision content beyond quantity-removal messages

decision_taken: getConfirmedObservationAssetQuery and listObservationRevisions gate on record time (hlc.physical <= asOf) because these reads are about what is KNOWN, while log listing gates on effective time (valid time) — two timelines, deliberately
reversal_condition: a reader finds the source using one timeline for both
